title: Awareness markets shortcut hijacks out-of-scope instrument queries (wheat/soybean/futures → canned BTC/ETH line)
source: Ash live normie session 2026-07-12 ('this has been a nightmare')
current_state: In normie mode (cfg.awareness_shortcut=True), try_answer_from_awareness routes any message containing a _MARKET_SIGNALS word to _extract_markets, which returned the snapshot's 'Markets:' line UNCONDITIONALLY. 'for futures trading and shyt, whats up with wheat and soybean' contains 'trading' (a market signal) -> _detect_topic returns 'markets' -> the user got the canned 'Markets: BTC-USD ... | ETH-USD ...' line instead of an actual wheat/soybean answer. The snapshot only carries crypto/index/stock tickers; it has no commodities, forex, or bonds. The news path already guards this exact class (T-160: business/finance and specific-entity queries return None so the LLM+tools handle them); markets never got the guard.
target_state: _extract_markets declines (returns None) when the user names a specific instrument the snapshot does not carry (commodities, forex, bonds, futures), so those defer to the LLM+tools. Generic market questions ('how's the market') and instruments actually present in the snapshot line still fire. Mirrors the T-160 news specificity guard.
resolution: Added _SPECIFIC_INSTRUMENT_WORDS frozenset (commodities/forex/bonds/futures) and a scope guard in _extract_markets: tokenize the user message and the snapshot Markets line; if the user names a specific-instrument word that is not a subset of the snapshot's tokens, return None. _extract_markets now takes user_lower (call site at line ~105 updated). 2 regression tests in test_awareness_shortcut.py: the exact wheat/soybean/futures/forex/bond cases fall through, generic + in-snapshot queries still fire.
migration_plan: 2. Guard in _extract_markets: named = message tokens & _SPECIFIC_INSTRUMENT_WORDS; if named and not named.issubset(snapshot Markets-line tokens) -> None.
3. Thread user_lower through the _extract_markets call site.
4. Regression tests: commodity/forex/bond/futures queries -> None; generic + in-snapshot -> fire.
risk_notes: Known adjacent gap left unfixed on purpose (out of scope): 'eth' is not in _MARKET_SIGNALS, so 'what's eth at' never reaches topic=markets even though the snapshot carries ETH-USD. Separate one-line fix if wanted. Also observed but separate: the snapshot's Markets line was crypto-only in Ash's session (no S&P/Nasdaq/Dow), so even a generic 'how's the market' fires with crypto-only data — that is an awareness-snapshot data-completeness issue in the snapshot builder, not this shortcut.
